site.name: Nornir
site.tagline: Turn personal archives into traceable memory.
site.description: Nornir imports personal source archives into MySQL, keeps evidence attached, and produces bounded handoffs for biography, search, and AI-assisted synthesis.

hero.title: Turn personal archives into traceable memory.
hero.body: Nornir is an evidence-first memory system. It imports mail, chats, social exports, health records, archived websites, and old message bases into clean Laravel models so later synthesis has receipts instead of vibes.

sections.title: Messy history goes in. Named records come out.
sections.body: Nornir treats each archive as a source with its own shape, not as generic text mulch.

items.title: Mail and threads
items.body: Gmail messages, labels, attachments, thread links, important-mail triage, and source-page handoffs.

items.title: Chats and exports
items.body: ChatGPT conversations, Apple Messages, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, and their source-specific media references.

items.title: Old networks
items.body: FidoNet areas, participants, messages, threads, cleanup notes, and observations kept separate from authored text.

items.title: Files and captures
items.body: Media collections, Apple Health records, Wayback captures, screenshots, extracted text, and provenance links.

sections.title: Some parts work. Some are still scaffolding.
sections.body: Nornir is the working storehouse today. The named companion layers are the direction of travel, not all finished product.

items.title: Nornir - working
items.body: Importers, canonical MySQL tables, runs, artifacts, provenance, search documents, and source handoffs are real code now.

items.title: Muninn - partial
items.body: Evidence-first biography work has early Gmail slices: important-mail bundles, candidate artifacts, and manual analysis notes.

items.title: Heimdallr - planned
items.body: The read-only access boundary is specified, but not built yet. Today, code still reads through models and services directly.

items.title: Mimir - planned
items.body: The presentation layer will handle UI, query surfaces, timelines, source exploration, graph views, and arc views.

items.title: Huginn - planned
items.body: The synthesis layer is specified, but future work. It must write only from traceable evidence, not confident smoke.

sections.title: The claim is not enough.
sections.body: Muninn work is evidence-first. Nornir keeps observations, source handoffs, artifacts, and provenance attached so future biography work can show where each claim came from.

items.title: Source handoffs
items.body: Each importer can write bounded artifacts that describe what a source page proves, what it suggests, and what still needs human judgment.

items.title: Observations
items.body: Derived facts live beside raw imported records. They do not overwrite the source or pretend interpretation is capture.

items.title: Provenance
items.body: Runs, artifacts, and links make it possible to trace a result back to source material instead of trusting a generated paragraph.

sections.title: Good fences. Fewer haunted conclusions.
sections.body: Nornir is deliberately boring at the storage boundary because personal memory work gets dangerous when the machinery starts improvising.

items.title: MySQL is canonical
items.body: Imported source material belongs in MySQL. Compiled markdown is output, not the source of truth.

items.title: Heimdallr is read-only
items.body: Access work can inspect. It does not mutate the memory store or smuggle writes through a convenient back door.

items.title: Huginn needs receipts
items.body: Synthesis can write, but not without traceable support. Pretty lies are still lies, just wearing nicer shoes.

pipeline.title: From archive dump to usable memory.

steps.title: Record intake
steps.body: Capture source labels, paths, importer choices, run metadata, and enough context to replay or explain the import.

steps.title: Import records
steps.body: Map each source into focused Laravel tables and models instead of flattening everything into vague blobs.

steps.title: Preserve evidence
steps.body: Keep attachments, media references, timestamps, participants, labels, URLs, and source-specific identifiers intact.

steps.title: Observe carefully
steps.body: Write derived observations as their own layer, with the source record still visible underneath.

steps.title: Build handoffs
steps.body: Produce bounded artifacts for source navigation, important evidence bundles, and biography candidate work.

steps.title: Search and synthesize
steps.body: Rebuild search documents and feed later Muninn or Huginn work with evidence, not an undifferentiated text swamp.

skills.title: Biography work needs a paper trail.
skills.body: Nornir is the storehouse below the synthesis layer. It gives Muninn enough structured evidence to write cautiously, revise cleanly, and avoid turning memory into fan fiction.

items.title: Candidate artifacts
items.body: Biography candidates can be built from important mail bundles and source handoffs, with support kept close.

items.title: Manual notes
items.body: Human analysis can be saved as artifacts instead of disappearing into chat history.

items.title: Traceable scope
items.body: Each handoff names the slice it covers. No pretending a few records explain a whole life.

aiHandoff.title: Give the model rails.
aiHandoff.body: Nornir does not ask an AI to rummage through raw exports and improvise. It builds scoped, evidence-backed handoffs so a model can help without quietly inventing the load-bearing parts.

items.title: Bounded context
items.body: Source-page handoffs keep the relevant records together and leave unrelated archive noise outside the prompt.

items.title: Explicit uncertainty
items.body: Artifacts can carry misses, gaps, and weak signals instead of sanding every edge into false confidence.

items.title: Reusable outputs
items.body: The same evidence can support search, biography, review, and later synthesis without starting from scratch every time.

showcase.title: Find the thread before pulling it.
showcase.body: Search documents are built from source-specific models. That keeps retrieval useful without erasing the structure that made the record meaningful.

frames.label: Mail
frames.title: Thread evidence
frames.body: Messages, labels, attachments, sender context, and important-mail scoring stay connected.

frames.label: Social
frames.title: Export structure
frames.body: Posts, reactions, comments, profile snapshots, and media references keep their source-specific roles.

frames.label: Archive
frames.title: Old-source context
frames.body: Wayback, FidoNet, and media imports keep enough provenance to explain where a record came from.

boundary.title: MySQL is canonical. Markdown is compiled output.
boundary.body: Nornir keeps imported source material in the database, writes compiled markdown under `wiki/`, and keeps private source dumps under ignored local storage. The public repo gets the machinery, not the diary.

nameStory.title: Threads, fate, memory. Fine, one myth reference.
nameStory.body: The Norns tend the threads. That fits a project that turns scattered personal archives into traceable memory without pretending the thread explains itself.

cta.title: Build from the receipts.
cta.body: Nornir is for the unglamorous part of personal AI work: importing the sources, keeping the boundaries clean, and making later synthesis answerable to evidence.

footer.text: Built as a static project site. No Laravel runtime. No Jekyll. No archive leakage.
